claimText: The Global Methane Status Report 2025 is jointly released by the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) and the Climate and Clean Air Coalition (CCAC).
evidenceExcerpt: The Global Methane Status Report 2025 was released by UNEP and the CCAC.
examUse: Statement-based question on reports and publishing organizations.

claimText: The Climate and Clean Air Coalition (CCAC) specifically targets short-lived climate pollutants, including methane, black carbon, hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs), and tropospheric ozone.
evidenceExcerpt: CCAC (founded 2012, convened within UNEP) targets short-lived climate pollutants: methane, black carbon, HFCs, and tropospheric ozone.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying which pollutants fall under CCAC's mandate.

claimText: India is a member of the Climate and Clean Air Coalition (CCAC), having joined the initiative in 2019.
evidenceExcerpt: India joined the Climate and Clean Air Coalition (CCAC) in 2019.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's participation in international climate initiatives.
distractorHooks: India is a founding member from 2012
India is not a member of CCAC

claimText: Methane is over 80 times more potent than carbon dioxide over a 20-year period but has a much shorter atmospheric lifespan of only 7 to 12 years.
evidenceExcerpt: Methane is over 80 times more potent than CO2 over a 20-year period but persists for only 7-12 years.
examUse: Statement-based question comparing greenhouse gas properties.
distractorHooks: CO2 is more potent than methane over a 20-year period

claimText: While 60 percent of methane emissions are anthropogenic, wetlands constitute the largest natural source of methane emissions.
evidenceExcerpt: 60% of methane emissions are anthropogenic; wetlands are the largest natural source.
examUse: Statement-based question on sources of greenhouse gases.
distractorHooks: Oceans are the largest natural source
Permafrost is the largest natural source

claimText: India is the world's third-largest emitter of methane, with its emissions primarily driven by agricultural activities such as stubble burning.
evidenceExcerpt: India is the world's 3rd largest methane emitter, primarily driven by stubble burning.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's emission profile.
distractorHooks: India is the largest emitter of methane
Emissions are primarily driven by fossil fuel extraction
